instructions: Judge whether the proposal is a good shallow bootstrap memory graph for this exact repo commit.
Do not compute a numeric score. Only classify expected nodes, expected edges, and quality issues.
Judge semantic equivalence, not exact IDs or exact wording.
A node can satisfy an expected item if it clearly represents the same concept, even with a different ID.
An edge can satisfy an expected relation if the proposal connects the same semantic subjects through inline compact fields or canonical edges.

Compact relationship fields are graph edges: flow.touches creates Flow -> Component touches edges, claim.about creates Claim -> Component/Flow about edges, component.contains creates Component -> Component contains edges, and flow.contains creates Flow -> Flow contains edges.
When judging expected edges, inspect compact relationship arrays directly. Do not require a separate creates.edges entry.

Do not mark a real existing source file or source folder as a bad anchor merely because it is broad. Bad anchors are fake paths, generated/dependency paths, .git paths, or useless anchors like the repo root.
Do not mark a combined top-level component as noisy merely because a more detailed rubric has separate expected nodes. Missing specificity belongs in node coverage, not quality penalties.
Penalize depth only when the proposal is clearly doing a deep audit instead of top-level bootstrap.
The proposal should be useful to a future coding agent that needs to understand this repo quickly.
actual_repo_facts: This repo is a small TypeScript project named greplica.
The package exposes a greplica binary that points to dist/apps/cli/main.js.
The TypeScript build compiles apps, libs, and evals into dist using tsconfig.build.json.
README.md documents Greplica setup, the greplica command, GREPLICA_HOME, and the two bundled skills.
apps/cli/main.ts is the CLI entrypoint and dispatches commands by argv.
apps/cli/main.ts imports createLocalKnowledgeGraphService from libs/knowledge-graph/service.ts.
apps/cli/main.ts imports detectRepoContext from apps/cli/repo-context.ts.
apps/cli/main.ts imports loadRepoEnv/envVarSource from libs/env/load-local-env.ts.
The CLI supports greplica init.
greplica init detects repo context, calls service.initRepo, and prints repo/default branch/database/scope information.
The CLI supports greplica doctor [--check-openai].
greplica doctor detects repo context, initializes memory, reports OPENAI_API_KEY source or missing setup, and can smoke-test OpenAI embeddings.
The CLI supports greplica graph read.
greplica graph read detects repo context, calls service.readGraph, and prints components, flows, claims, sources, and edges.
The CLI supports greplica graph context <query>.
greplica graph context requires a non-empty query, calls service.contextGraph, and prints graph context JSON.
The CLI supports greplica proposal validate <file>.
greplica proposal validate reads a proposal JSON file, calls service.validateProposal, and reports validation errors.
The CLI supports greplica proposal apply <file>.
greplica proposal apply reads a proposal JSON file, calls service.applyProposal, prints created object counts, and reports embedding counts.
The CLI is intentionally a thin local client: argument routing, file reading, repo detection, env loading, and human text output live there.
apps/cli/repo-context.ts uses git commands to detect the repo root.
apps/cli/repo-context.ts reads remote.origin.url when available and falls back to local:<repoRoot>.
apps/cli/repo-context.ts derives repo_name from the remote URL or local root basename.
apps/cli/repo-context.ts derives default_branch from refs/remotes/origin/HEAD and falls back to main.
libs/env/load-local-env.ts loads OPENAI_API_KEY from the process environment, repo-root .env.local, or repo-root .env without overwriting existing env values.
libs/knowledge-graph/service.ts defines KnowledgeGraphService as the boundary the CLI calls for graph operations.
KnowledgeGraphService.initRepo upserts a repo and ensures a main scope and a working scope.
The main scope is named with the repo default branch.
The working scope is named working and parented to the main scope.
KnowledgeGraphService.readGraph loads the repo by remote URL and asks the repository for the current graph view.
KnowledgeGraphService.contextGraph loads the repo by remote URL and builds a ranked graph context result for the query.
KnowledgeGraphService.validateProposal normalizes compact proposal input before validating it.
KnowledgeGraphService.applyProposal normalizes the proposal, validates it, requires the working scope, creates a memory commit, writes proposal records, and ensures graph-object embeddings.
createLocalKnowledgeGraphService wires KnowledgeGraphService to SqliteRepository and openDatabase.
libs/knowledge-graph/graph-context/context-builder.ts builds graph context results over claims, components, and flows.
Graph context retrieval combines semantic embeddings, BM25, exact matching, and graph relationship boosts.
Graph context embeddings use OpenAIEmbedder and require OPENAI_API_KEY when missing embeddings must be created.
libs/knowledge-graph/proposal.ts defines the canonical MemoryCommitProposal shape.
A proposal can create components, flows, claims, sources, and edges.
proposal.ts also defines compact proposal types that allow relationships inline on components, flows, and claims.
Compact component relationship fields include contains and supersedes.
Compact flow relationship fields include contains, touches, and supersedes.
Compact claim relationship fields include about, evidenced_by, and supersedes.
normalizeProposal removes compact relationship fields from nodes and converts them into canonical Edge records.
normalizeProposal generates deterministic edge IDs from kind/from/to fields and deduplicates generated edges by edge ID.
libs/knowledge-graph/validate-proposal.ts validates proposal structure, graph object fields, endpoint existence, and allowed edge directions.
Allowed claim kinds are fact, requirement, decision, task, question, and risk.
Allowed claim truth values are code_verified, source_verified, and unknown.
Allowed claim intent values are intended, accidental, and unknown.
Allowed source kinds currently include session, prd, doc, issue, pr, artifact, and code.
libs/knowledge-graph/edge.ts defines allowed edge kinds: about, contains, touches, supersedes, and evidenced_by.
libs/knowledge-graph/schema.ts defines graph IDs, Component, Flow, Source, GraphObjectType, and MembershipSubjectType.
libs/knowledge-graph/claim.ts defines ClaimKind, ClaimTruth, ClaimIntent, and Claim.
libs/knowledge-graph/scope.ts defines GraphScopeKind, GraphScope, and GraphMembership.
libs/knowledge-graph/commit.ts defines MemoryCommit.
libs/storage/sqlite/db.ts opens the local SQLite database.
The default SQLite database lives under GREPLICA_HOME when set.
If GREPLICA_HOME is not set, libs/storage/sqlite/db.ts still accepts legacy ENGINEERING_CONTEXT_HOME before falling back to ~/.greplica.
libs/storage/sqlite/schema.ts defines tables for repos, graph_scopes, memory_commits, components, flows, claims, sources, edges, graph_memberships, and graph_object_embeddings.
The graph_memberships table links scope_id, subject_type, subject_id, and memory_commit_id.
MembershipSubjectType excludes source; memberships are for component, flow, claim, and edge.
SqliteRepository.upsertRepo creates deterministic repo IDs from the remote URL.
SqliteRepository.ensureScope creates deterministic scope IDs from repo, kind, and name.
SqliteRepository.requireWorkingScope finds the working scope for a repo.
SqliteRepository.createMemoryCommit creates a new memory commit and links it to the previous commit in the same scope.
SqliteRepository.createProposalRecords inserts graph objects and creates graph memberships for components, flows, claims, and edges.
SqliteRepository.createProposalRecords inserts sources globally without graph memberships.
SqliteRepository.readGraphView reads current main and working scopes, loads active memberships, filters inactive edges, and removes superseded subjects from the active view.
SqliteRepository stores graph_object_embeddings keyed by repo, object type, object id, provider, model, and dimensions.
skills/greplica-bootstrap/SKILL.md bootstraps initial Greplica memory for a repository.
skills/greplica-update-working-memory/SKILL.md updates Greplica working memory from a coding-agent session.
The bootstrap skill tells agents to run greplica doctor, inspect the repo shallowly, create high-signal memory, validate it, and apply it.
The update skill tells agents to inspect session changes, reuse existing memory with greplica graph context, store durable decisions/facts/tasks, validate, and apply.
The skills say bootstrap memory should be shallow and high-signal, not exhaustive.
The skills say sources are external artifacts and should not be created just because code was inspected during bootstrap.

expected_nodes.description: Greplica CLI app that exposes doctor, init, graph read/context, and proposal validate/apply commands.
Repo context detection from Git root, remote URL, repo name, and default branch.
Repo-local environment loading for OPENAI_API_KEY from env, .env.local, and .env.
KnowledgeGraphService boundary for init, read, context retrieval, validation, and apply.
Graph context retrieval and embedding/ranking implementation.
Proposal model and compact relationship normalization.
Proposal validation for graph object fields, allowed values, references, and edge directions.
Knowledge graph schema/types for components, flows, claims, sources, edges, scopes, memberships, commits, and embeddings.
SQLite database opener, schema, migrations, repository, graph view, memory commits, and embeddings persistence.
Bundled Greplica bootstrap and working-memory update skills.
Doctor/init flow detects repo context, loads env, initializes memory state, and reports setup readiness.
Graph read/context flow reads the composed graph and builds ranked context for a query.
Proposal validation flow normalizes compact proposal input and validates graph shape.
Proposal apply flow validates, creates a memory commit, writes records to working scope, and ensures embeddings.
Compact relationship fields become canonical graph edges.
Greplica skills guide shallow bootstrap and durable working-memory update workflows.
CLI is a thin local client over KnowledgeGraphService and repo/env setup helpers.
Doctor initializes memory, reports OPENAI_API_KEY source, and optionally checks OpenAI embeddings.
Repo context detection derives remote URL, repo name, and default branch from Git with fallbacks.
Database path prefers GREPLICA_HOME, accepts legacy ENGINEERING_CONTEXT_HOME, then defaults to ~/.greplica.
KnowledgeGraphService owns repo init, graph read/context, proposal validation, and proposal apply operations.
Graph context combines semantic embeddings, BM25, exact matching, and graph relationship boosts.
Proposal apply writes to the working scope through a memory commit and ensures graph-object embeddings.
Compact proposal fields normalize into canonical edges.
Validation enforces allowed claim values, source kinds, edge kinds, endpoint existence, and allowed edge directions.
Graph read/context use active records from main plus working and remove superseded subjects from the active view.
Sources are global and graph memberships exclude sources.
Greplica skills instruct agents to create shallow high-signal bootstrap memory and durable session updates, then validate/apply proposals.

expected_edges.description: Doctor/init flow touches the CLI app.
Doctor/init flow touches repo context detection.
Doctor flow touches env loading for OPENAI_API_KEY.
Doctor/init flow initializes memory through the service and storage.
Graph context flow touches graph context retrieval.
Graph read/context flow touches SQLite storage for graph views and embeddings.
Proposal validation flow touches proposal model/normalization and validation.

expected_edges.to: KnowledgeGraphService, SQLite storage, and graph schema components
expected_edges.description: Proposal apply flow touches service, storage, memory commits/schema, and graph records.
Proposal apply ensures embeddings used by graph context retrieval.

expected_edges.to: proposal model and graph schema components
expected_edges.description: Compact relationship flow touches proposal normalization and graph edge/schema model.

expected_edges.to: Greplica skills, CLI, proposal, and graph context components
expected_edges.description: Skill workflow touches the Greplica skills and the CLI/proposal/context commands they instruct agents to use.
Important claims are about relevant components and/or flows rather than disconnected.

quality_rules.noisy_structure_nodes: Components and flows that do not help top-level repo orientation, such as package-lock, tsconfig-only nodes, every tiny type file, generic source-code component, or single-helper-function flow. Do not count a real top-level subsystem as noisy only because it groups related files.
quality_rules.bad_claims: Claims that are wrong, unsupported, duplicated, vague, too low-level, or not useful for future engineering work. Doctor support, GREPLICA_HOME, the legacy ENGINEERING_CONTEXT_HOME fallback, repo-local OPENAI_API_KEY loading, and graph-object embeddings are real current facts for this pinned commit.
quality_rules.bad_anchors: Anchors that point to fake paths, generated output, dependency folders, .git, or a vague repo root without a strong reason. A real file or folder anchor is acceptable if it points to the code where the represented component lives.
quality_rules.depth_violation: The proposal is clearly too deep for bootstrap, such as component-per-file graphing, function-level tracing, or a full code audit.
quality_rules.weak_graph_linking: The proposal creates nodes but does not meaningfully connect flows to components and claims to components/flows.
